TextENGNew: "...I see.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1303. No, no, you don't need to file anything.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1304.Satoshi-kun, you're a valued member of the Hinamizawa Fighters.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1305.Your spot will be waiting for you.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1306.You can come back whenever you're ready, okay?"
changeReason: New translation adds 'Satoshi-kun' address and 'file anything' (closer to JPN 届け/filing a form), and changes 'promising' to 'valued'; old omitted Satoshi's name and the filing context.

TextENGNew: "I'm not sure about that...@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1033. I don't think I'll be able to come back for a while, so I thought it would be better to formally withdraw..."
Satoshi-kun had come to tell me he wanted to leave the Hinamizawa Fighters.
I was well aware of the circumstances surrounding Satoshi-kun and Satoko-chan,@k so I had considered the possibility that he might say something like this...
The rift between Satoko-chan and her aunt had apparently become fatal—so severe that reconciliation was impossible.
changeReason: Old says 'to the point that it can be considered abuse' while new says 'so severe that reconciliation was impossible'; JPN 修復不可能なほどに酷い means 'so bad it's beyond repair' -- the old incorrectly introduces 'abuse' not in the Japanese.

TextENGNew: Recently, someone from the child consultation center had come to intervene, and the aunt briefly behaved herself during that visit.@k ...But immediately after, the harassment resumed in an even more insidious form.
Satoko-chan's weekly checkup numbers had been gradually deteriorating,@k and we were barely keeping her stable by increasing her medication.
......Unless her relationship with her aunt improved, she would eventually relapse into L5 symptoms and sink into madness.
changeReason: Old says 'go terminal' generically; new specifies 'relapse into L5 symptoms' matching the Japanese Ｌ５の症状をぶり返し, preserving the specific medical terminology.

TextENGNew: ...Though, if I recall correctly, her terminal symptoms were not the deranged type.
...She was the type to harbor the madness within, keeping it hidden beneath a composed exterior.
...Just as she had pushed her parents off the cliff two years ago... there was a chance Satoko-chan might kill her aunt.
Thanks to the Mountain Dogs' intervention, the fall had been ruled an accident and Ooishi had backed off, though he himself remained suspicious.
...If another incident like that occurred around Satoko-chan... he would surely investigate her without mercy.
...The deterioration of Satoko-chan's environment had already reached a point beyond anything I could do.
So it was not surprising that Satoshi-kun, who cared so deeply for his sister, would want to quit baseball to be constantly at her side...
"Actually, I'm starting a part-time job.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1035. So I won't be able to make it to practice..."
"...A part-time job?@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1308. Is there something you want to buy?"
"...Yes.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1037. Satoko's birthday is coming up soon.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1038.There's a stuffed animal she's been wanting, and I'd like to get it for her."
changeReason: Old translates ぬいぐるみ as 'teddy bear' while new correctly uses 'stuffed animal'; ぬいぐるみ is a general term for any stuffed toy, not specifically a teddy bear.

TextENGNew: Now that he mentioned it, that was right.
changeReason: Old 'That's right' is a simple affirmation; new 'Now that he mentioned it, that was right' captures the JPN そう言えばそうだっけ nuance of being reminded of something previously known.

TextENGNew: I recalled the date written on her insurance card.
June 24th, if I remembered correctly.
Right after the Watanagashi festival.
"I see.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1310. ...You really are a wonderful big brother, Satoshi-kun.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1311.I'm sure Satoko-chan will be thrilled."
"......I hope she likes it."
From the way Satoshi-kun spoke, it was clear he was carrying an enormous amount of stress.
...For him, seeing Satoko-chan suffer was no different from suffering himself...
The proper remedy for such strain was moderate exercise through sports and regular, adequate sleep.
If Satoko-chan was being berated by her aunt night after night, then Satoshi-kun, who shielded her, was almost certainly not getting enough rest.
...I did not know what kind of job he would be taking, but unlike sports, it was unlikely to help him release stress.
...Frankly, I could not approve of adding a physically and mentally taxing job on top of the exhaustion Satoshi-kun was already carrying.
I offered to help pay for the present, but naturally, given his character, he would not accept.
It was his gift to give, he said, so it was only right that he earn every yen himself. He declined with quiet resolve.
The part-time job was solely to earn enough for Satoko-chan's present, so it would apparently be short-term.
...If it was only for a brief period, perhaps it would not push him too far...

In truth, I wanted to prevent him from piling on more burdens during a time when his heart was already strained.@k But with Satoko-chan's birthday approaching, persuading a brother so devoted to his sister was all but impossible.
......Satoshi-kun's resolve was firm.
Pressing him any further to give up the job might damage the trust between us.
Fearing that outcome, I accepted his leave of absence and resolved to watch over him.
"...Oh, Coach.@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1041. ...Could I maybe borrow a bat?"
I completely misread the meaning behind his words.
...Later, when I learned he had been carrying that bat as a weapon for self-defense,@k I realized that even at this point, he had already been exhibiting dangerous warning signs.
He had become convinced that the series of mysterious deaths would claim a victim again this fourth year, and that the victim would be him.
changeReason: Old says 'a fourth mysterious death this year' (suggesting the fourth individual death) while new says 'the series of mysterious deaths would claim a victim again this fourth year'; JPN 連続怪死事件が四年目 refers to the fourth year of the ongoing series.

TextENGNew: ...An obsessive delusion that someone was out to take his life.
That was unmistakably a symptom nearing the terminal stage of Hinamizawa Syndrome.
...And yet, despite being someone deeply involved with the syndrome, I had been inexcusably careless...
"A bat?@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1313. What would you need a bat for?"
Satoshi-kun did have a bat of his own.
So rather than asking to "borrow" it, the proper request would have been asking to "take it home." But he specifically said "borrow."
...In hindsight, seeking my permission to take it was a way of lightening his guilty conscience. But there was no way I could have known that then...
"Well...@k@vS20/07/VTK1_satoshi1043. I won't be able to come to practice much... but I think I can at least find time to practice my swings on my own."
"That's wonderful!@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1315. By all means, take it with you.@k@vS20/10/VTK1_irie1316.Just make sure to check your surroundings carefully before swinging, so no one gets hurt.
"If it won't bother the neighbors, hanging a tire and hitting it makes for excellent practice."
Even just swinging a bat would surely help relieve stress.
And so, letting him take his bat home would ease his burden, however slightly.
...That was what I believed.
............But... that was a grave mistake.
I would learn the truth only when things had already passed the point of no return...
